text.title: client-conventions-reverse-engineering Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the client-conventions-reverse-engineering methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Extract from explicit configs FIRST (lint/formatter/tsconfig/pyproject). Only infer implicit conventions when explicit ones are missing.
rule.rationale: Explicit configs are ground truth; inferred patterns can be wrong.

rule.statement: Sample at least 20 recent merged PRs when inferring implicit conventions. Smaller samples produce noisy guardrails.
rule.rationale: Below 20 the inferred pattern is dominated by one author's style.

rule.statement: Output is a single Markdown file (AGENTS.md / repo-rules.md / CLAUDE.md style). Do not split conventions across multiple docs.
rule.rationale: Agents read one file; multiple split docs cause inconsistency.

rule.statement: Each rule cites a source (lint config path OR PR # / commit SHA). No 'always do X' without a source.
rule.rationale: Without source, the rule decays into folklore.

rule.statement: Version + last_reviewed metadata required. Re-run extraction every 30 days or after a PR-rejection cycle.
Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
